DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO ENFORCE ADMINISTRATIVE SUBPOENAS IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 5, 2017 TO GIVE THE PARTIES AN OPPORTUNITY TO ENGAGE IN FURTHER MEET AND CONFER EFFORTS TO NARROW OR POSSIBLY ELIMINATE THE ISSUES IN DISPUTE. IN ADDITION, THE COURT GRANTED PETITIONER'S ORAL REQUEST TO FILE SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS. PETITIONER CCSF MAY FILE ITS BRIEF ON 10/02/17; RESPONDENTS MAY FILE A RESPONSE NO LATER THAN 10/04/17. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR#6576, RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO ENFORCE ADMINISTRATIVE SUBPOENAS IS GRANTED IN PART AND CONTINUED IN PART TO DECEMBER 19, 2017 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302/LAW AND MOTION. THE HEARING IS CONTINUED FOR COMPLIANCE STATUS ON ITEMS 1 THROUGH 6 AND FOR FURTHER BRIEFING AND ARGUMENT ON ITEMS 7 AND 8. UBER SHALL FILE AN ADDITIONAL MOTION TO BY NOVEMBER 27, 2017; OPPOSITION THERETO BY DECEMBER 8, 2017; REPLY BY DECEMBER 15, 2017. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. [SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS.] JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ken Walczak, Deputy City Attorney and Austin Yang, Deputy City Attoney 415-54-6761 for Petitioner City and County of San Francisco.
Sanjay Nangia, Esq. 415-276-6577 of Daivs Wright Tremaine LLP for Respondent.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Hearing is held. Plaintiff inquires re: shape of the case, reports status of the case and compliance with the subpoena. Parties to meet and confer after 2/14/18 re: compliance. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoenas. The Court sets a compliance date of March 8, 2018 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. Parties to inform the Court if matters have been resolved prior to the hearing. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR# 7489, mgheno@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoenas
Off Calendar Nov-16-2017 Continued To Dec-19-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Deputy City Attorneys, Austin M. Yang and Kenneth M. Walczak of the San Francisco City Attoryney's Office (415-554-6761) appear as counsels for petitioner/moving party, City and County of San Francisco

AttorneyS, Sanjay Nangia and Rochelle L. Wilcox of the law offices of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP (415-276-6577) appear as counsels for respondents/opposing parties, Uber Technologies, Inc., Razier LLC et al.

Court directs counsel to meet and confer. The matter is passed until the end of calendar call. Court and counsel discuss the various issues in this motion in light of appeal proceedings and a potential stay, as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Uber waives argument as to items 1 through 6 and the court issues orders as set forth on the record.

PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoenas is granted in part and CONTINUED in part to December 19, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 302/Law and Motion. The hearing is continued for compliance status on items 1 through 6 and for further briefing and argument on items 7 and 8. Uber shall file an additional motion to by November 27, 2017, covering whether the appeal by Uber stays in whole
or in part, this court's ability to issue orders as to item 7 and 8. Court tentatively finds that items 7 and 8 are within the purview of a potential stay. Briefing/filing schedule is as follows: Uber's additional moving papers by November 27, 2017; Opposition thereto by December 8, 2017; Reply by December 15, 2017. Hearing shall also be December 19, 2018 at 9:30 AM in Department 302/Law and Motion. Counsel discuss items 1 through 6 and the Court issues enforcement orders as follows. As to items 1 and 2, Court orders full compliance by February 14, 2018. As to items 3, 4 and 6 Uber shall either file a declaration regarding inability to comply or comply fully by January 5, 2018. As to item 5, Uber shall file a high level declaration why they cannot comply or comply fully by February 14, 2018. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Reding, CSR No. 9711, e-mail: lreding@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoenas
Off Calendar Oct-05-2017 Continued To Nov-16-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Deputy City Attorneys, Kenneth M. Walczak and Austin Yang of the San Francisco City Attorney;s Office (415-554-4206) appear as counsels for petitioner/moving party, City and County of San Francisco

Attorney, Allison A. Davis of the law offices of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP (415-276-6580) appears as counsel for respondent/opposing party, Uber Technologies, Inc.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

A request for relief from "Attorney Eyes Only" designation is DENIED. Court orders Uber to produce P.U.C. reports on or before November 20, 2017, unless an extension is agreed-upon by the City.

PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoenas is Continued for further hearing as to items 1-7 on November 16, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 302. Further notice is waived.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura A. Reding, CSR No. 9711, e-mail: lreding@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoenas
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Continued To Oct-05-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kenneth M. Walczak, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.4206, for petitioner CCSF;
Sanjay Nangia, Esq. and Allison A. Davis, Esq. of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP, Tel. 415.276.6577, for respondents UBER Technologies, et al.

PETITIONER CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoenas was discussed. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Petitioner City and County of San Francisco's motion to enforce administrative subpoenas is continued to October 5, 2017 to give the parties an opportunity to engage in further meet and confer efforts to narrow or possibly eliminate the issues in dispute. In addition, the Court granted petitioner's oral request to file supplemental briefs. Petitioner CCSF may file its brief on 10/02/17; respondents may file a response no later than 10/04/17. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org.
